title: quercus minor
intro: Quercus minor, also known as the dwarf oak or little oak, is a small evergreen tree or shrub native to East Asia, belonging to the Quercus genus. The medicinal properties of Quercus minor include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins. The health benefits of Quercus minor include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Quercus minor can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as tea, decoction, or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
